content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


October 11, 1990


Frederick K. Lowell
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
Post Office Box 7880
San Francisco, CA 94120

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-581

Dear Mr. Lowell:

You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice
provided to you on behalf of Pacific Telesis Group under the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your letter correctly summarizes the advice provided to you
concerning the exception in Section 82015 for a payment made by an
occupant of a home or office for costs related to a meeting or
fundraiser held in the occupant's home or office, for which the
fundraiser held in the occupant's home or office, for which the copy
costs are $500 or less. (Section 82015; Regulation 18215(d), copy
enclosed.)

However, we revise our telephone advice in the following
respects:

(1) With regard to holding the candidate events in "an
auditorium or meeting room that is used by the company for similar
events but is also made available to the public after hours and
meeting rental fee," the fair market value of the auditorium or meeting
room rental must be included as a "contribution" which counts
toward the $500 threshold in the exception set out in
Gross Advice Letter, No. A-83-200; Newman
Regulation 18215. (Gross Advice Letter, No. A-84-187, copy enclosed.)

(2) With regard to the issue of whether costs in connection
with communications to Pacific Telesis employees announcing the
events are "contributions" which must be counted toward the $500
threshold for the exception in Regulation 18215:


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660


Frederick K. Lowell
Page Two

     If the announcement is part of a regularly published
newsletter which complies with the criteria set out in Regulation
18225(b)(4)(C) (as described on page 3 of your letter), the costs
in connection with the announcement are not "contributions," even
though the communications are made at the behest of the
candidates.

     If Pacific Telesis Group sends the announcement about the
speaking events to its employees as a separate mailing (i.e., not
as a part of its regularly published newsletter), the amount of
the in-kind contributions will be the fair market value of
preparing and sending the announcement.  Generally, the amount of
an in-kind contribution in the form of a communication is whatever
it would cost the candidate or committee at whose behest it is
made to send the communication.

     Please call me a (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Jeanne Pritchard
                                             Chief, Technical Assistance
                                             and Analysis Division

embedding.qa_text: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** October 11, 1990 Frederick K. Lowell Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro Post Office Box 7880 San Francisco, CA 94120 Re: Your Request For Advice Our File No. A-90-581 Dear Mr. Lowell: You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on behalf of Pacific Telesis Group under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Your letter correctly summarizes the advice provided to you concerning the exception in Section 82015 for a payment made by an occupant of a home or office for costs related to a meeting or fundraiser held in the occupant’s home or office, for which the costs are $500 or less. (Section 82015; Regulation 18215(d), copy enclosed.) However, we revise our telephone advice in the following respects: (1) With regard to holding the candidate events in "an auditorium or meeting room that is used by the company for similar events but is also made available to the public after hours for a rental fee," the fair market value of the auditorium or meeting room rental must be included as a "contribution" which counts toward the $500 threshold in the exception set out in Regulation 18215. (Gross Advice Letter, No. A-83-200; Newman Advice Letter, No. A-84-187, copy enclosed.) (2) With regard to the issue of whether costs in connection with communications to Pacific Telesis employees announcing the events are "contributions" which must be counted toward the $500 threshold for the exception in Regulation 18215: 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. The image you provided is completely white, with no visible text or content. If you have another page or another image to share, please provide it for better results. Frederick K. Lowell Page Two If the announcement is part of a regularly published newsletter which complies with the criteria set out in Regulation 18225(b)(4)(C) (as described on page 3 of your letter), the costs in connection with the announcement are not "contributions," even though the communications are made at the behest of the candidates. If Pacific Telesis Group sends the announcement about the speaking events to its employees as a separate mailing (i.e., not as a part of its regularly published newsletter), the amount of the in-kind contributions will be the fair market value of preparing and sending the announcement. Generally, the amount of an in-kind contribution in the form of a communication is whatever it would cost the candidate or committee at whose behest it is made to send the communication. Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel Jeanne Pritchard By: Jeanne Pritchard Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
